data.title: GIBO Holdings announces transfer to Nasdaq Capital Market
data.description:
• GIBO Holdings (NASDAQ:GIBO) on Friday said that it has received approval from the Nasdaq Listing Qualifications department of The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC to transfer the listing of the company's Class A ordinary shares from the Nasdaq Global Market to the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • The shares will be transferred to the Nasdaq Capital Market effective as of the open of business on October 20, 2025, and will continue to trade under the symbol "GIBO."
  • Meanwhile, the company's warrants to purchase Class A ordinary shares will remain listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol "GIBOW."
  • GIBO -1.37% after hours to $2.515.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Federal worker jobless claims jump to highest level since 2019 shutdown
data.description:
Jobless claims from U.S. federal workers surged to their highest mark since the 2019 government shutdown, according to data from state unemployment insurance program offices released Friday.

For the week ended Oct. 11, there were 7,224 initial claims filed in the Unemployment Compensation for Federal Employees program, more than doubling from the prior week and the highest since early 2019.

During a government shutdown, the Labor Department pauses its weekly jobless claims reports, though states and territories can continue submitting their data.

Several states were not included in the latest week of data, including Arizona, Massachusetts, Nevada, Tennessee or the Virgin Islands.

It was recently reported that the Trump administration has sent layoff notices to thousands of federal employees across multiple agencies, amid the shutdown, which is now stretching into its 17th day.

Federal staffers placed on furlough during a shutdown may qualify for unemployment benefits, depending on state rules. In most cases, those benefits must be repaid once back pay is issued, according to a fact sheet.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: BP says fire extinguished at Midwest's largest refinery
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) said Friday it extinguished a fire at its 440,000 bbl/day Whiting refinery in Indiana, the largest in the U.S. Midwest, that caused gasoline prices to surge in parts of the region.

The refinery was actively flaring early on Friday, and multiple units at the refinery were offline, Reuters reported, citing an alert to clients from Wood Mackenzie that said the 255,000-bbl/day crude distillation unit, associated 60,325 bbl/day vacuum distillation unit, 100,000 bbl/day cat feed hydrotreater, and 24,300 bbl/day hydrotreater were shut during the early morning, and the 115,000 bbl/day fluid catalytic cracker was shut Thursday night.

The crude distillation unit could be down for at least a week, Reuters reported, citing sources.

"Great Lakes spot gasoline prices spiking on the BP refinery fire overnight, could lead to prices cycling soon. For now, wholesale prices pointing to about a 20c/gal rise," GasBuddy's Patrick De Haan said in a post.

The incident occurred two months after Whiting took multiple units offline for more than a week in August due to flooding caused by severe thunderstorms.

data.title: Liberty Energy outlines over 1 gigawatt power capacity target through 2027 as completions business moderates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Liberty Energy Inc. (LBRT) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ron Gusek reported that Liberty achieved revenue of $947 million and adjusted EBITDA of $128 million in the third quarter, citing a slowdown in completions activity and market pricing pressure. Gusek highlighted: “Our team delivered solid operational results, once again delivering the highest combined average daily pumping efficiency and safety performance in Liberty's history.”
  • Gusek indicated continued emphasis on technology: “Our digiPrime fleets are achieving outstanding performance and leading efficiency metrics across the company. Several fleets deployed with our largest customers broke new records for pumping hours, horsepower hours and proppant volumes pumped during the quarter.” He noted measurable cost improvements from the digiPrime pumps and stated “total maintenance cost savings are greater than 30% on digiPrime pumps.”
  • Discussing automation and digital platforms, Gusek said, “Fleet automation is driving a 65% improvement in the time to deliver the desired fluid injection rate and a 5% to 10% improvement in hydraulic efficiency.”
  • On power business expansion: “We are in the process of securing additional power generation, bringing our total capacity to over 1 gigawatt to be delivered through 2027, and we expect further increases will be necessary to meet the growing demand for our services.”
  • Gusek announced, “We recently welcomed Alice Yake, a recognized energy and infrastructure expert, to our Board to help guide and accelerate our efforts in power services.”
  • CFO Michael Stock stated: “In the third quarter of 2025, revenue was $947 million, compared to $1 billion in the prior quarter. Our results decreased 9% sequentially as activity softened following a strong uptick in the second quarter and market-driven pricing headwinds took hold.”

Outlook

  • Gusek described the anticipated moderation in activity as “transitory in nature,” citing global oil oversupply peaking in the first half of 2026 and a favorable trajectory for long-term gas demand and completions activity. He added, “Many shale oil producers are targeting relatively flat oil production, requiring modest activity improvement in the coming year from current levels.”
  • Stock projected: “We now expect to have approximately 500 megawatts of generation delivered by the end of '26 and over 1 gigawatt of cumulative power generation by the end of '27. We expect further increases will be necessary to meet significant power opportunities while our completions CapEx moderates in the years ahead.”
  • Management expects typical seasonal trends in the fourth quarter and “no significant cash taxes in the fourth quarter.”

Financial Results

  • The company reported third quarter revenue of $947 million and net income of $43 million. Adjusted net loss was $10 million, and fully diluted net income per share was $0.26, with adjusted net loss per diluted share at ($0.06). Adjusted EBITDA came in at $128 million.
  • General and administrative expenses totaled $58 million, flat with the prior quarter. The company ended the quarter with a cash balance of $13 million and net debt of $240 million. Net capital expenditures were $113 million in the third quarter.
  • The company increased its quarterly cash dividend by 13%.

Q&A

  • Stephen Gengaro, Stifel: Asked about visibility on power generation demand and customer base. Gusek responded that the sales pipeline “has more than doubled from what we talked about at the end of the second quarter,” and that urgency has “increased meaningfully.” He added, “between LOIs and contract terms, we have out in front of customers paper for more than a few gigawatts of capacity needs.”
  • Marc Bianchi, TD Cowen: Inquired about financing for power capacity. Stock explained, “Those project companies will be funded via debt that is backed by that PPA, ESA…maybe around -- you could get to approximately 70% of the capital needs by debt. It will be nonrecourse to the corporation.”
  • Scott Gruber, Citigroup: Asked about CapEx buildup and EBITDA payback on contracts. Stock detailed, “Installed generation around about $1.5 million, $1.6 million a megawatt.” On payback, “probably talking 5, 5-and-a-bit on that…shorter-term contracts…will be on the 3-year version.”
  • Saurabh Pant, BofA Securities: Sought details on risk management with long-term power contracts. Stock explained the importance of counterparty selection and project structure: “Each one of these projects, large load projects, will be rolled down into a separate project code, as I said, with nonrecourse debt.”
  • Derek Podhaizer, Piper Sandler: Asked about equipment mix for new capacity. Gusek replied, “The vast majority of this incremental capacity is also gas recip. Turbines will play a role…we will still lean very heavily on the gas recip technology.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on power contract visibility, capital allocation, and risk management, maintaining a probing but constructive tone. Their questions focused on execution timelines, financing structure, and technology mix, indicating a neutral to slightly positive sentiment.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone, repeatedly emphasizing long-term strategy, risk controls, and unique market positioning. Gusek and Stock both used phrases such as “we are confident,” “we have thought through all that very, very carefully,” and “we are well positioned,” signaling steady confidence despite near-term headwinds.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone was more focused on navigating industry softness and emphasizing the emerging power business, with a consistent message of cautious optimism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted to a sharper emphasis on expanding the power business, with a clear target of over 1 gigawatt capacity by 2027, compared to more exploratory commentary in the prior quarter.
  • Management’s tone pivoted from celebrating outperformance in Q2 to stressing resilience amid market softness in Q3, while highlighting structural advantages and technology leadership.
  • Analysts shifted attention from completions activity and supply chain questions in Q2 to deeper scrutiny of the power business’s risk, financing, and execution pace in Q3.
  • Key metrics declined sequentially, with revenue, EBITDA, and net income all lower than the previous quarter, and completions CapEx signaled to moderate further.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward power generation and technology integration, with less emphasis on fleet expansion in completions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited industry pricing pressure, lower completions activity, and the ongoing need for contract conversion in the power business.
  • In response to analyst concerns about execution risk and capital allocation, Stock outlined a disciplined project finance approach and emphasized focusing on investment-grade counterparties.
  • Gusek noted that the pace of power contract conversion is slower than the core oilfield services business and described the process as “long in the making.”

Final Takeaway

Liberty Energy’s third quarter call signaled a decisive shift in strategic focus, with management underscoring the company’s transition toward large-scale power generation projects targeting over 1 gigawatt of capacity by 2027. While industry pricing headwinds and a slowdown in completions weighed on quarterly results, management emphasized operational efficiency, differentiated technology, and a robust power business pipeline as drivers of future value. The company reinforced its commitment to prudent capital allocation and long-term partnerships, while highlighting a strengthened board and increased dividend as evidence of confidence in its evolving business model.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Google likely to release Gemini 3 model in December: report
data.description:
• Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) is likely to release the next version of its artificial intelligence large language model, known as Gemini 3, in December, technology journalist Alex Heath reported.
  • The search giant has historically released new base versions of its Gemini models in December, the most recent being Gemini 2.0. The latest model from Google — Gemini 2.5 Pro — was released in March. An updated version of the model, Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview (I/O edition), which contained improved capabilities for coding, was announced in June at the tech giant's I/O developer event.
  • Alphabet, Google's parent company, said on its most recent earnings call that the Gemini app had surpassed 450M monthly active users.
  • Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Clear Channel Outdoor jumps om report Mubadala Capital exploring takeover
data.description:
Clear Channel Outdoor (NYSE:CCO) soared 27% after a report that Mubadala Capital is exploring a purchase of the billboard company.

The asset management arm of Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund Mubadala Investment Co. has been working on a potential purchase of Clear Channel Outdoor, according to a  Bloomberg report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

Talks are ongoing, and Mubadala may decide not to go forward with a deal, according to the report. Clear Channel has a market cap of over $700 million and debt of about $6.4 billion.

Mubadala Capital and Clear Channel declined to comment to Bloomberg.

News of a potential sale comes as activist investor Anson Funds Management has been pushing the outdoor advertising company to pursue a sale.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Tesla and Netflix leads an earnings blitz, Samsung event, and delayed CPI
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Sunday - October 19

  • History check - It is the 38th anniversary of Black Monday when the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 22.6% and the S&P 500 Index cratered 20.5%.

 

Monday - October 20

  • Volatility watch - Reddit (RDDT) and Biohaven (BHVN) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Investview (OTCQB:INVU), Praxis Precision (PRAX), and Vine Hill Capital Investment (VCIC). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Jin Medical (ZJYL), Graphic Packaging (GPK), and Tvardi Therapeutics (TVRD). Hertz Global (HTZ) and SoundHound AI (SOUN) head into the week with a high level of short interest outstanding.
  • Politics watch - The government shutdown and uncertainty around the U.S.-China trade negotiations will take center stage once again.
  • IPO watch - The IPO is slowing down due to the impact of the government shutdown, with no new debuts expected in the week ahead. The analyst quiet period expires on Knorex (KNRX) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Caterpillar (CAT), Dell Technologies (DELL), CVS Health (CVS), and Procter & Gamble (PG).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include W.R. Berkley (WRB), Steel Dynamics (STLD), and Crown Holdings (CCK).
  • All day - China's Communist Party will hold the Fourth Plenum to review the party's five-year plan.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date for Glaukos Corporation's (GKOS) Epioxa (Epi-on), a corneal cross-linking therapy for keratoconus.
  • All day - The UK CMA Phase 1 review of Getty Images's (GETY) planned acquisition of Shutterstock (SSTK) expires.

 

Tuesday - October 21

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Netflix (NFLX), GE Aerospace (GE), Coca-Cola (KO), General Motors (GM), Philip Morris (PM) Lockheed Martin (LMT), Mattel (MAT), Texas Instruments (TXN), and Capital One (COF). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Galaxy Digital (GLXY) and Elevance Health (ELV) after they report.
  • All day - Samsung Galaxy (OTCPK:SSNLF) will hold its Worlds Wide Open media event. The company is expected to reveal its new XR headset, known internally as Project Moohan.
  • All day - Robinhood Markets (HOOD) CEO Vlad Tenev is one of the notable speakers scheduled for the TED AI Conference in San Francisco. Executives from Oracle (ORCL), HPE, and eBay (EBAY) will also participate.
  • All day - Disney (DIS) will raise the prices of its Disney+, Hulu, and ESPN streaming services. The new pricing affects both standalone and bundled plans across the United States.
  • All day - WTI crude November futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • All day - Shareholders with MeridianLink (MLNK) vote on the takeover offer from Centerbridge.
  • 9:00 a.m. Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller will give opening remarks before the Federal Reserve Board Payments Innovation Conference.
  • 9:00 a.m. Philip Morris (PM) will hold its earnings call. Supplier Universal Corp (UVV) derives 16% of its revenue from Philip Morris and trades in tandem with it 88% of the time on earnings day.

 

Wednesday - October 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Tesla (TSLA), AT&T (T), IBM (IBM), Lam Research (LRCX), Boston Scientific (BSX), Las Vegas Sands (LVS), and Hilton Worldwide (HLT). Options trading implies a double-digit move for QuantumScape (QS) and Winnebago (WGO) after they report.
  • All day - Shareholders with Cenovus Energy (CVE) and MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF) vote on the proposed merger between the companies.
  • All day - The AACR-NCI-EORTC International Conference on Molecular Targets and Cancer Therapeutics will begin. The highly watched annual conference has the primary goals of fostering international collaboration, accelerating the exchange of innovative research, presenting the latest in drug development, and updating early-stage cancer clinical trials. Some of the notable companies participating include Xencor (XNCR), Nuvalent (NUVL), and Zentalis Pharmaceuticals (ZNTL).
  • All day - Construction Partners (ROAD) will host an in-person Analyst Day and hot-mix asphalt facility tour with financial analysts and institutional investors.
  • 5:30 p.m. Tesla (TSLA) will hold its earnings call. Elon Musk's update on the robotaxi service rollout could have implications for Waymo (WAYMO) and Uber Technologies (UBER). Options trading implies a share price swing of more than 8% after the report is released.

 

Thursday - October 23

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include T-Mobile (TMUS), Intel (INTC), Union Pacific (UNP), Honeywell (HON), and Ford Motor (F). Options trading implies a double-digit share price move for Alaska Air (ALK) and Deckers Outdoor (DECK) after they report.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date for GSK's (GSK) Belantamab mafodotin (Blenrep) in relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.
  • All day - The World Coffee Innovation Summit in London will take place. Starbucks (SBUX), Lavazza, and JDE Peet's are some of the notable companies participating.
  • 10:00 a.m. The Existing Home Sales report will be released by the National Association of Realtors.

 

Friday - October 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Procter & Gamble (PG), General Dynamics (GD), and HCA Healthcare (HCA).
  • All day - A flurry of economic reports are expected to be released, including the September CPI report, PMI data, the new home sales report, and the University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment update.

 

Seeking Alpha editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article.

data.title: Tech Voices: Stellantis-Pony.ai robotaxis, Trump-Putin Tunnel, Oracle
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of announcements, statements, and remarks that could impact the technology industry. 

  • China's Pony.ai (NASDAQ:PONY) and Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) are teaming up to develop SAE Level 4 robotaxi vehicles.

The EVs will feature Stellantis's AV-Ready Platform and Pony.ai's autonomous driving technology. The companies' first vehicle will be based on the Peugeot e-Traveller, with an initial launch planned for Luxembourg in the next few months, followed by a gradual rollout in other European cities next year.

"We are delighted to partner with Stellantis to bring our autonomous mobility technology to Europe," said Pony.ai founder and CEO Dr. James Peng, in a statement. "Their strong European presence and portfolio of iconic brands make them the ideal partner to help accelerate our growth in this key market."

Based in Amsterdam, Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) owns several legacy car brands, including Jeep, Chrysler, Dodge, Peugeot, and Fiat. 

Other companies involved in the development of robotaxi services include Tesla (TSLA), Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Waymo, Amazon's (AMZN) Zoox, Uber (UBER), Lyft (LYFT), and Baidu (BIDU). 

  • A key Russian investment envoy has proposed that Elon Musk's The Boring Company build an undersea rail tunnel to connect Siberia and Alaska. 

"Imagine connecting the US and Russia, the Americas and the Afro-Eurasia with the Putin-Trump Tunnel - a 70-mile link symbolizing unity. Traditional costs are $65B+, but @boringcompany's tech could reduce it to <$8B. Let's build a future together! " said Kirill Dmitriev, the head of Russia’s sovereign wealth fund, in a series of posts detailing the proposed project on X. 

U.S. and Russian officials are expected to meet next week to discuss a possible end to the Ukraine war. 

  • American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) regional carrier Envoy Air is the latest victim of a hacker attack targeting Oracle's (NYSE:ORCL) E-Business Suite applications.

“We have conducted a thorough review of the data at issue and have confirmed no sensitive or customer data was affected,” an Envoy Air spokesperson told Reuters. “A limited amount of business information and commercial contact details may have been compromised.”

Reuters said the hack is the latest targeting Oracle's E-Business Suite by the cybercriminal group CL0P. The hacker group listed American Airlines as one of its victims on its website Thursday.
